items.title: Compliment
items.meaning: Meaning: To praise someone
items.example: Example: He complimented her dress

items.title: Complement
items.meaning: Meaning: To complete or enhance something
items.example: Example: The scarf complemenrs her outfit

items.title: Empathy
items.meaning: Meaning: Ability to understand and feel what another person is experiencing
items.example: Example: She showed empathy by listening to her friend without judging

items.title: Sympathy
items.meaning: Meaning: Feeling sorry or concerned for someone without fully sharing their emotions
items.example: Example: She expressed sympathy for her friend after the loss of her pet

items.title: Apathy
items.meaning: Meaning: Lack of interest,concern,or emotion toward someone else's situation
items.example: Example: His apathy towards his studies worried his parents

items.title: Further
items.meaning: Meaning: Refers to additional or extra,or to continue
items.example: Example: I need to discuss further before making a decision

items.title: Farther
items.meaning: Meaning: Refers to physical distance
items.example: Example: The park is farther than I thought from my house

items.title: Expect
items.meaning: Meaning: To believe something will happen
items.example: Example: I expect good results this year

items.title: Accept
items.meaning: Meaning: To receive or agree to something
items.example: Example: She accepted the job offer happily

items.title: Except
items.meaning: Meaning: To leave out or not include
items.example: Example: Everyone came except John

items.title: Affect
items.meaning: Meaning: To influence
items.example: Example: Lack of sleep can affect your mood

items.title: Effect
items.meaning: Meaning: Result or outcome
items.example: Example: The medicine had a good effect on her health

items.title: Impact
items.meaning: Meaning: Strong effect or influence
items.example: Example: The speech had a big impact on the audience

items.title: Emigrate
items.meaning: Meaning: To leave your own country to live in another
items.example: Example: She emigrated from India to Canada

items.title: Immigrate
items.meaning: Meaning: To come into a new country to live there
items.example: Example: He immigrated to Australia for work

items.title: Migrate
items.meaning: Meaning: To move from one place to another
items.example: Example: Many people migrate to cities for better opportunities

items.title: Ellicit
items.meaning: Meaning: To bring out or draw out
items.example: Example: The teacher's question elicited a thoughtful answer

items.title: Illicit
items.meaning: Meaning: Illegal or forbidden
items.example: Example: They were arrested for illicit drug trade

items.title: Explicit
items.meaning: Meaning: Very clear and direct;leaving now doubt
items.example: Example: She gave explicit instructions for the task

items.title: Through
items.meaning: Meaning: From one side to the other
items.example: Example: She walked through the door

items.title: Though
items.meaning: Meaning: However
items.example: Example: It was raining,though we went out

items.title: Thorough
items.meaning: Meaning: Complete or detailed
items.example: Example: She did a thorough job on the project

items.title: Assure
items.meaning: Meaning: To remove doubt
items.example: Example: She assured me that everything would be fine

items.title: Ensure
items.meaning: Meaning: To make sure something happens
items.example: Example: Please ensure the door is locked

items.title: Insure
items.meaning: Meaning: To protect financially against loss or damage
items.example: Example: They insured the car against accident

items.title: Optimist
items.meaning: Meaning: A person who expects positive outcomes and focuses on the good
items.example: Example: This is a setback,not the end

items.title: Pessimist
items.meaning: Meaning: A person who expects negative outcomes and focuses on problems
items.example: Example: Nothing ever works out for me

items.title: Personal
items.meaning: Meaning: Related to an indivdual or private life
items.example: Example: Please do not use your personal phone during office hours

items.title: Personnel
items.meaning: Meaning: People employed in an organisation; Staff
items.example: Example: Only authorisd personnel are allowed beyond this point

items.title: Peak
items.meaning: Meaning: The highest point or maximum level
items.example: Example: She reached the peak of her career at just 35

items.title: Peek
items.meaning: Meaning: To take a quick or secret look
items.example: Example: He took a peek at the surprise gift

items.title: Pique
items.meaning: Meaning: To stimuate interest or curiosity
items.example: Example: The trailer piqued my interest

items.title: Allusion
items.meaning: Meaning: An inirect reference to something
items.example: Example: His speech made an allusion to Romeo and Juliet

items.title: Iluusion
items.meaning: Meaning: Something that appears real but is actually false
items.example: Example: The magician created the illusion of disappearing

items.title: Delusion
items.meaning: Meaning: A strong belief in something that is not true
items.example: Example: He was under the delusion that he couldn't fail

items.title: Eminent
items.meaning: Meaning: Famous,respected or highly regarded
items.example: Example: She is an eminent cardiologist known worldwide

items.title: Imminent
items.meaning: Meaning: About to happen very soon
items.example: Example: Rain is imminent

items.title: Prominent
items.meaning: Meaning: Important,noticeable or well-known
items.example: Example: She plays a prominent role in the company

items.title: Cite
items.meaning: Meaning: To quote,mention or refernce as evidence
items.example: Example: Please cite your sources in the report

items.title: Site
items.meaning: Meaning: A place or location
items.example: Example: The construction site is closed

items.title: Sight
items.meaning: Meaning: Vision or something seen
items.example: Example: He lost his sight after the accident

items.title: Apprehend
items.meaning: Meaning: Arrest or catch
items.example: Example: The police apprehended the thief

items.title: Comprehend
items.meaning: Meaning: To understand
items.example: Example: I finally comprehend the concept

items.title: Reprehend
items.meaning: Meaning: Strongly criticize
items.example: Example: The manager reprehended the mistake

items.title: Persecute
items.meaning: Meaning: To treat someone cruelly or unfairly,often because of religion,race or belief
items.example: Example: Many people were persecuted for their religious beliefs

items.title: Persecute
items.meaning: Meaning: To legal action against someone in court
items.example: Example: The state decided to prosecute the company for fraud

items.title: Execute
items.meaning: Meaning: To carry out a plan
items.example: Example: The team executed the stratergy perfectly
